task.objective: Add pagination support (offset parameter) to MCP tools that currently only support limit-based queries. This enables AI assistants and clients to efficiently navigate large datasets without fetching everything at once.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - MCP Server Tools
context.background:
The MCP server exposes tools for querying test execution data. Several tools support a `limit` parameter but lack `offset` support, making it impossible to paginate through large result sets. This is particularly problematic for:
- Organizations with thousands of test executions - Tests with extensive history (100+ runs) - Search queries that match many tests
Without pagination, clients must either: 1. Request very large limits (memory/performance issues) 2. Accept incomplete data (missing older results)
context.audience: - AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor, etc.) - Developers building MCP client integrations
context.impact: HIGH - Enables efficient data access patterns for large organizations. Reduces memory usage and improves response times for subsequent pages.

tool.name: get_executions
tool.current_params: limit, status, branch, suite, from, to
tool.use_case: Browse execution history page by page

tool.name: search_tests
tool.use_case: Paginate through search results when many tests match

tool.name: get_test_history
tool.use_case: View older runs of a specific test beyond the first page

tool.name: get_failed_tests
tool.use_case: Paginate through failures when there are many

functional.requirement: Add `offset` parameter to all affected tools
Default offset to 0 for backwards compatibility
Update database functions to accept offset parameter
Modify SQL queries to include OFFSET clause
Include pagination metadata in responses (has_more, total_count)
Add cursor-based pagination as alternative (future enhancement)

non_functional.requirement: Maintain backwards compatibility - existing calls without offset must work
No performance regression for first page queries (offset=0)

solution.approach: 1. Update database functions (lib/db.ts) to accept optional offset parameter 2. Modify SQL queries to include OFFSET clause 3. Update tool definitions (lib/mcp/tools.ts) with new offset parameter 4. Update tool handlers to pass offset to database functions 5. Optionally add pagination metadata to responses

change.signature_before: getExecutions(organizationId: number, limit?: number, status?: string, branch?: string, dateRange?: DateRangeFilter, suite?: string)
change.signature_after: getExecutions(organizationId: number, limit?: number, offset?: number, status?: string, branch?: string, dateRange?: DateRangeFilter, suite?: string)

change.signature_before: searchTests(organizationId: number, query: string, limit?: number)
change.signature_after: searchTests(organizationId: number, query: string, limit?: number, offset?: number)

change.signature_before: getTestHistory(organizationId: number, signature: string, limit?: number)
change.signature_after: getTestHistory(organizationId: number, signature: string, limit?: number, offset?: number)

change.update_description: Add to description: "Use offset for pagination (e.g., offset=20 with limit=20 shows results 21-40)."

response_enhancement.note: has_more can be computed as: results.length === limit If results.length < limit, there are no more results.

test.description: No existing unit tests for MCP tools

verification.test: No dedicated unit tests exist. Consider adding in future iteration.

steps.step: 2. Use Claude Code or MCP client to call get_executions with limit=5
3. Note the results and verify count is 5
6. Verify has_more is true if 5 results returned
7. Continue with offset=10, etc. until has_more is false

test.expected: Each page shows different results. Last page has has_more=false.

test.script:
# First page curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_executions", "args": {"limit": 5, "offset": 0}}'
# Second page curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_executions", "args": {"limit": 5, "offset": 5}}'
# Verify different IDs in each response

steps.step: 3. Verify response structure is compatible (has count, executions)

implementation_order.step: Update getExecutions function signature and SQL query to accept offset
Update searchTests function signature and SQL query
Update getTestHistory function signature and SQL query
Add offset parameter to tool definitions (allTools array)
Update tool handlers to validate and pass offset parameter
Add pagination metadata to responses (optional enhancement)

always.rule: Default offset to 0 for backwards compatibility

never.rule: Never make offset a required parameter
Never change the order of existing function parameters

files_to_modify.file: lib/mcp/tools.ts - Update 4 tool definitions and handlers
